<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

This pull request makes several configuration and functionality updates to the E2E testing infrastructure and email handling. It removes an E2E test that verified OpenAPI schema generation, updates Playwright configuration to enforce single-worker execution and fixed timeouts across all environments, and introduces new E2E npm scripts for database initialization and development workflows. The package.json is updated to load environment variables from both `.env` and `.env.example` files, and dependencies are bumped for better-upload, TanStack Start, and Zustand. A new server environment variable `EMAIL_DELIVERY_DISABLED` is added to control email sending behavior. Request headers are now passed to permission checks in middlewares and upload handlers, and the `GuardAuthenticated` component refactors navigation logic into a `useEffect` hook. A new Vite plugin is introduced to serve JSON files as importable JavaScript modules during development.

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the E2E testing infrastructure to use a shared PGLite database instance, updates several core dependencies, and introduces a new environment variable to disable email delivery. It also adds a custom Vite plugin to handle JSON imports during development and refactors the `GuardAuthenticated` component to perform navigation within a `useEffect` hook. The review feedback suggests using the `useLocation` hook from `@tanstack/react-router` instead of the global `location` object to ensure consistency and SSR compatibility, as well as ensuring the dependency array for the new effect is complete.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Import `useLocation` from `@tanstack/react-router` to access the current location in a way that is consistent with the router state and safe for server-side rendering (SSR).

```suggestion
import { useRouter, useLocation } from '@tanstack/react-router';
```

**Actionable comments posted: 2**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@src/features/auth/guard-authenticated.tsx`:
- Around line 22-24: The render guard and the effect use inconsistent predicates
(render returns null when !session.data?.user but the effect only redirects when
session.error.status > 0), causing a blank state when session.error exists
without a positive status; fix by making the redirect/effect and the render use
the same predicate: use session.isPending, session.error (truthy), and
session.data?.user consistently. For example, update the effect and render to
derive a single boolean (e.g., const isUnauthenticated = !session.isPending &&
(!session.data?.user || !!session.error)) and use that in both the navigation
logic and the component return so any session.error triggers the redirect or the
same UI path. Ensure you change the checks that reference session.isPending,
session.error, and session.data?.user so they match exactly in both places.

In `@vite.config.ts`:
- Around line 34-37: The call to decodeURIComponent(url.pathname) can throw a
URIError for malformed percent-encodings and needs to be guarded so the
middleware doesn't crash; wrap the decode in a small safeDecode (or inline
try/catch) around decodeURIComponent(url.pathname) used to build filePath,
handle URIError by falling back to url.pathname (or returning/continuing early)
and then use the decoded/ fallback value when constructing filePath with
server.config.root; ensure any error is contained so the surrounding try/catch
can handle downstream logic.
```

</details>

<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit for review status -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Verify the decode call is currently outside a protective try/catch block.
rg -n -C4 'decodeURIComponent\(url\.pathname\)' --type=ts
rg -n -C4 'try \{' --type=ts vite.config.ts
```

Repository: hbmartin/start-ui-web

Length of output: 726

---



</details>

**Guard `decodeURIComponent` to prevent uncaught `URIError` in middleware.**

Malformed URL-encoded paths throw `URIError` before your `try/catch` block at line 44, causing the middleware to fail and potentially destabilizing the dev server.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-        const filePath = path.resolve(
-          server.config.root,
-          `.${decodeURIComponent(url.pathname)}`
-        );
+        let decodedPathname: string;
+        try {
+          decodedPathname = decodeURIComponent(url.pathname);
+        } catch {
+          next();
+          return;
+        }
+
+        const filePath = path.resolve(
+          server.config.root,
+          `.${decodedPathname}`
+        );
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@vite.config.ts` around lines 34 - 37, The call to
decodeURIComponent(url.pathname) can throw a URIError for malformed
percent-encodings and needs to be guarded so the middleware doesn't crash; wrap
the decode in a small safeDecode (or inline try/catch) around
decodeURIComponent(url.pathname) used to build filePath, handle URIError by
falling back to url.pathname (or returning/continuing early) and then use the
decoded/ fallback value when constructing filePath with server.config.root;
ensure any error is contained so the surrounding try/catch can handle downstream
logic.
```

</details>
